metadata.title:
SATURDAY EQUAL

: BY MRS. MINNIE J. REYNOLDS.

pages.raw_text:
Down in the southeast corner of
Washington there Is one town that
is bound to 60 for equal suffrage
next election day, anyway. This is
Charleston. Charleston is on one side
the Snake river, in Washington, and
Lewiston is JUS across the river, in
Idaho. In Lewiston the women can
vote and in Charleston they can't.
There is a bridge between those
- towns, and women at one end


THAT bridge can vote and at the
other end they can't. If you walk
across that bridge into Idaho you
find that the women know enough
to vote; but II you walk across into
Washington you find that they don't
know enough to vote. Now this ia
funny, and whatever else may be
alleged against the American man,
he is never accused of lacking a
sense Of humor. And that's the rea-
son they all say in Charleston that
that town has got to 60 for suffrage
if.SH another one in the state does.


SThere is woman in Spokane
who is part owner in one of the big
business blocks Of the city. Last
winter when the great suffrage Pc
titiOn to congress was in circula.
ton, she asked a colored janitor in
